beats.narration_text: This is Musinique. Guten Tag — Ask Claude to create a plugin, scaffold a plugin structure, understand directory layout, set up plugin.json, or organize plugin components — the Plugin Structure skill fires. Claude Code plugins follow a standardized layout: a manifest in the dot-claude-plugin directory, and component directories — commands, agents, skills, hooks — at the plugin root. Auto-discovery means you add a file to the right directory and it loads; no registration step. Use dollar-sign CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT for every path reference in hooks and MCP config so the plugin works regardless of where it's installed. Let me walk you through the directory layout, the five component types, and where this skill gets it right and where it bites.

props.topic: MUSINIQUE · PLUGIN STRUCTURE · CLAUDE CODE SKILL
props.command: Create a plugin that reviews code and runs tests — what structure do I need?
output: Manifest: .claude-plugin/plugin.json — only required field is name
Components at root: commands/ agents/ skills/ hooks/ — auto-discovered
All paths: ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/... — never hardcode absolute paths

beats.narration_text: The directory layout. Every plugin has a root directory with this structure: a .claude-plugin subdirectory holding plugin.json — the manifest — and component directories at the plugin root level: commands for slash commands, agents for subagent definitions, skills for SKILL.md-based skills in subdirectories, hooks for hooks.json and its scripts, .mcp.json for MCP server definitions, and scripts for shared utilities. The two critical rules: the manifest must be in .claude-plugin, not at the root; and the component directories must be at the root, not inside .claude-plugin. This mistake is common and silent — misplacing either means components don't load. The manifest, plugin.json, has one required field: name, in kebab-case. Everything else is optional recommended metadata: version following semantic versioning, description, author, homepage, repository, license, keywords. Custom paths in the manifest supplement auto-discovery — they don't replace it. If you set a custom agents path, the default agents directory is still scanned. The only naming rules: kebab-case everywhere for all directories and files.

props.sparkLine: plugin.json in .claude-plugin/. Components at root. Never inside .claude-plugin/.

beats.narration_text: You don't need a 400-line system prompt to manage your release calendar. You need a 15-line core that knows what the job is, and skill modules it loads when the job gets specific — the release checklist, the pitch template, the playlist-scam detector. Decompose once, and the machine gets cheaper every run. That's the same arithmetic whether you're running inventory for a warehouse or release cycles for a back catalog.

artifactLines: The same tools the platforms use — pointed back at you.
Roll your own. Own the deck.

beats.narration_text: Five component types. Commands: markdown files in the commands directory with YAML frontmatter — any .md file auto-loads as a slash command. Agents: markdown files in the agents directory — auto-loaded, invokable manually or selected automatically by Claude Code based on task context. Skills: each skill gets its own subdirectory inside skills, and must have a file named SKILL.md — not README.md, not skill.md, exactly SKILL.md — for auto-discovery. Hooks: a hooks.json configuration file in the hooks directory, with scripts in a scripts subdirectory; hooks register automatically on plugin enable. MCP servers: a .mcp.json file at the plugin root, or inline in plugin.json. The portable path rule: use dollar-sign CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT in every hook command, MCP server argument, and script reference. Never use hardcoded absolute paths, never use relative paths from the working directory, never use tilde expansions. Plugins install in different locations depending on how users install them — only CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT resolves correctly in all contexts. The variable is available as an environment variable inside hook scripts.

props.sparkLine: Commands slash. Agents auto. Skills need SKILL.md. Paths need ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}.

beats.act: teardown — what it gets right / where it bites
beats.narration_text: Here is the teardown. Plugin Structure gets auto-discovery right: commands, agents, and skills all load automatically from their directories — no registration step, no imports, just put the file in the right place. The CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT variable is clearly documented as required for all intra-plugin paths, with an explicit list of what never to use — absolute paths, working-directory relatives, home shortcuts. Three layout patterns — minimal, full-featured, skill-focused — cover the practical range. The troubleshooting section maps common failures to causes: component not loading, path resolution errors, auto-discovery not working. Where it bites: the manifest-in-.claude-plugin versus components-at-root rule is the most common mistake and gets one mention in Critical Rules but is never reinforced in the component sections. Custom paths supplementing rather than replacing defaults is easy to miss and leads to double-loading when both a custom path and the default directory exist. The skill states no restart required for changes but the hook-development skill says restart is required — inconsistent guidance for the same lifecycle event. SKILL.md is the required filename for skill discovery and a misnamed file produces silent failure — this is buried in the skills subsection. And command name collisions between two plugins produce silent conflicts with no resolution mechanism documented.

props.sparkLine: Auto-discovery solid. Placement rule: both silent and fatal.

beats.narration_text: Verdict. Plugin Structure fires for creating plugins, understanding directory layout, setting up plugin.json, or organizing components. Manifest in .claude-plugin/plugin.json — required field: name in kebab-case. Components at plugin root: commands/ (slash commands .md), agents/ (.md), skills/ (SKILL.md subdirs), hooks/hooks.json, .mcp.json. Auto-discovery: no registration step, just file in the right place. ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} for all path references — never hardcode. Custom paths supplement defaults, not replace. Gaps: .claude-plugin vs root placement rule silent on failure; supplement not replace easily missed; restart inconsistency with hook-development; SKILL.md exact name undiscoverable; command collision no resolution.

artifactLines: Manifest: .claude-plugin/plugin.json — name required, all else optional metadata
Components at ROOT: commands/ · agents/ · skills/ · hooks/ · .mcp.json
Auto-discovery: .md → command/agent, SKILL.md subdir → skill, hooks.json → hooks
${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} for all paths in hooks/MCP — never hardcode absolute paths
Custom paths supplement defaults — both directories scan, can double-load
Gaps: placement rule silent · SKILL.md exact name · restart inconsistency · collision

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Open a Claude Code session. Paste this: Create a plugin called code-review-assistant that has a review command, a code-reviewer agent, and an api-testing skill. Watch four things: first, does Claude place the manifest at .claude-plugin/plugin.json, not at the plugin root? Second, does Claude place commands, agents, and skills directories at the plugin root, not inside .claude-plugin? Third, does the skill directory contain a file named exactly SKILL.md — not README.md, not skill.md? And fourth, does any hook configuration use dollar-sign CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT for its command path rather than a hardcoded or working-directory-relative path? If the manifest is at plugin.json at the root — placement is wrong. If a directory called .claude-plugin/commands exists — components are misplaced. If the skill file is named anything other than SKILL.md — it will not be discovered. Those are your gates.

props.command: Create a plugin called code-review-assistant with a review command, code-reviewer agent, and api-testing skill.
props.runningText: Watch: .claude-plugin/plugin.json? components at root? SKILL.md exact? ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}?
output: Expected: .claude-plugin/plugin.json · commands/ agents/ skills/ at root
Red flag: manifest at root · components in .claude-plugin/ · README.md instead of SKILL.md
